specific_location: Bottom of Cottonwood Canyon on the initial bench above the riparian trees. I was on the SW side of the main drainage, about 200-400 ft from the Las Animas county line
description: While the two birds had apparently been foraging in a patch of open ground in the bottom of Cottonwood Canyon, I did not observe them until they flushed.  However, I did see them almost immediately and had a good view from about 25-30 feet, at eye level. I was able to observe that they were small doves (specifically, smaller than Mourning Doves), with long tails that were rounded, not pointed. I focused on the tails and noted white outer rectrices their full length. Also, rufous primaries were easily seen. I am familiar with INDO through time spent in Mexico.Behaviors: The birds flushed from the ground, where they had apparently been foraging in the bare areas between clumps of grass, and flew away at 5-10 feet above the groundCall: NonePlumage: Adult
